walkthrough.title: Choose Your Feature Film
walkthrough.text: Welcome to Final Girl! Before we set anything up, you need to pick a Killer and a Location. Final Girl uses a modular Feature Film system -- each Feature Film box has one Killer and one Location. The cool part is you can mix and match any Killer with any Location if you own multiple Feature Films. For your first game, just pick one Feature Film box and use both the Killer and Location from that box. Pop off the box covers -- the reverse sides are your Killer board and Location board.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Final Girl
walkthrough.text: Now grab a Final Girl card. You will find these inside the Feature Film boxes the first time you open them, but they should be stored in the Core Box for future games. Each Final Girl has a unique health value and an Ultimate Ability she can unlock by saving enough victims. For your first game, pick whoever sounds fun or just grab one at random. Place your Final Girl card below the Player board, face up with the Victim Saved spaces showing.

walkthrough.title: Lay Out the Player Board
walkthrough.text: Take the double-sided Player board from the Core Box and place it on the table. One side is normal difficulty, the other is Extreme Horror Mode. For your first few games, use the normal side. The Player board has a Health Track on the left, a Horror Track in the middle, a Time Track on the right side, and slots for your Hands and Backpack on the far right. There is also a Dead Victims space where killed victims will go.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Dice and Action Cards
walkthrough.text: Take the six custom dice from the Core Box and set them aside where you can reach them. Now find the Action cards. Pull out the six Zero Cost Action cards -- you can identify them by the gold hourglass icon with a zero in the lower right corner. These are your starting hand. Sort the remaining Action cards into piles by name and lay them out in two rows. This display is called the Action Tableau. Some piles will have two cards, others just one.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Victims and Meeples
walkthrough.text: Set the yellow Victim meeples, the Special Victim meeples (white, orange, and blue), and the three Victim Holding boards with their matching tokens aside for now. Also take the Final Girl meeple and the two Killer meeples out of the Core Box. Keep all remaining markers and tokens handy -- you will need them during the Feature Film setup.

walkthrough.title: Place the Killer and Location Boards
walkthrough.text: Place the Killer board above the Player board. Then place the Location board directly to the right of the Killer board. These two boards together form the top half of your play area. The Location board shows the map of interconnected spaces where your Final Girl, the Killer, and victims will move around.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Finale and Dark Power
walkthrough.text: Find the Killer's Finale cards -- they have card backs showing the Killer's upper body. Shuffle them and place one face down on the Killer board in the marked Finale space. Now find the Dark Power cards -- they have card backs showing the Killer's lower body. Shuffle them and place one face down below the Finale card. Put the remaining Finale and Dark Power cards back in the box without looking at them.

walkthrough.title: Build the Terror Deck
walkthrough.text: Take the Killer's Terror cards and the Location's Terror cards and shuffle them together into one combined deck. Deal out exactly 10 cards face down -- this is your Terror deck. Place it to the right of the Location board. Set the remaining Terror cards aside. The Terror deck is basically a timer. When it runs out, the Finale is revealed and things get really intense.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Item Decks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the Location's Item cards together and deal three piles of four cards each, placing them face down above the Location board. Flip the top card of each pile face up so you can see what is available at each search location. Put any remaining Item cards back in the box. Each pile corresponds to one of the orange Search spaces on the Location board.

walkthrough.title: Draw a Setup Card
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the Location's Setup cards and draw one. This card tells you exactly where to place the Final Girl meeple, the Killer meeple, and how many yellow Victim meeples go in each space. Follow the card precisely -- the icon with a person shows where the Final Girl starts, the skull icon shows where the Killer starts, and the numbers tell you how many victims go in each space. Keep the Setup card face up above the Location board for reference.

walkthrough.title: Place the Final Health Tokens
walkthrough.text: This is an important step. Take the nine black Final Health tokens from the Core Box and mix them around face down -- the sides with the +1 icon should be showing. Place one on the Killer board's circle and one on the Player board's circle WITHOUT LOOKING AT THEM. Put the remaining seven black tokens back in the box, still without peeking. These tokens create suspense -- when the Killer or Final Girl takes their last hit, you flip the token to see if they are truly dead or if they come back to life.

walkthrough.title: Set Health Markers
walkthrough.text: Place Health markers on the left side of both the Killer board and the Player board. Each should have Health markers equal to their maximum health, counting the black Final Health token. For example, if your Final Girl has 5 health, place the black Final Health token plus 4 normal Health markers. The Killer's max health is shown on the Killer board. Fill up both health tracks.

walkthrough.title: Set Bloodlust, Time, and Horror
walkthrough.text: Place the Bloodlust marker at the very bottom of the Bloodlust track on the right side of the Killer board -- the Killer starts at their weakest. Place the Time marker on the blue 6 space on the Player board's Time track. Finally, place the second Killer meeple on the Horror track on the Player board -- this meeple is now the Horror marker. Set it to the Killer's starting Horror Level, which is the number shown next to the icon in the upper left corner of the Killer board.

walkthrough.title: Draw the First Event
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the Location's Event cards and place them face down next to the Location board. Draw the top card and follow its instructions. This might add new victims to the board, introduce an ongoing effect, or do something else entirely. Events are thematic surprises that make each game feel different. You are now ready to begin!

summary.title: Choose Your Feature Film
bullets: Pick a Killer and a Location from your Feature Film boxes
You can mix and match any Killer with any Location
Remove the box covers -- the reverse sides are the game boards

summary.title: Choose Your Final Girl
bullets: Pick a Final Girl card (stored in Core Box after first game)
Each has unique health and an Ultimate Ability
Place face up below the Player board with Victim Saved spaces showing

summary.title: Lay Out the Player Board
bullets: Normal side for first games, Extreme Horror Mode on the reverse
Health Track (left), Horror Track (center), Time Track (right)
Hands and Backpack slots on the far right, Dead Victims space

summary.title: Set Up the Dice and Action Cards
bullets: Set 6 custom dice aside
Take the 6 Zero Cost Action cards as your starting hand
Sort remaining Action cards by name into the Action Tableau (two rows)

summary.title: Set Up Victims and Meeples
bullets: Set aside yellow Victim meeples and Special Victim meeples (white, orange, blue)
Set aside 3 Victim Holding boards with matching tokens
Take the Final Girl meeple and 2 Killer meeples from the Core Box

summary.title: Place the Killer and Location Boards
bullets: Killer board goes above the Player board
Location board goes to the right of the Killer board
Location board shows interconnected spaces for movement

summary.title: Set Up the Finale and Dark Power
bullets: Shuffle Finale cards, place 1 face down on the Killer board
Shuffle Dark Power cards, place 1 face down below the Finale
Return remaining cards to box without looking

summary.title: Build the Terror Deck
bullets: Combine Killer Terror cards + Location Terror cards, shuffle
Deal exactly 10 cards face down = the Terror deck
Place to the right of the Location board; set rest aside

summary.title: Set Up the Item Decks
bullets: Shuffle Location's Item cards
Deal 3 piles of 4 cards each, face down above the Location board
Flip the top card of each pile face up

summary.title: Draw a Setup Card
bullets: Shuffle Setup cards, draw 1
Place Final Girl meeple, Killer meeple, and Victims per the card
Keep Setup card face up for reference

summary.title: Place the Final Health Tokens
bullets: Mix 9 black Final Health tokens face down (do NOT peek)
Place 1 on Killer board circle, 1 on Player board circle -- secret
Return remaining 7 to box without looking

summary.title: Set Health Markers
bullets: Fill Killer's health track: black Final Health token + normal markers = max health
Fill Player's health track the same way using Final Girl card's health value
Example: 5 health = 1 black token + 4 normal markers

summary.title: Set Bloodlust, Time, and Horror
bullets: Bloodlust marker at bottom of Killer's Bloodlust track
Time marker on blue 6 space on Player board
Horror marker (second Killer meeple) on Horror track at Killer's starting level

summary.title: Draw the First Event
bullets: Shuffle Location's Event cards face down next to Location board
Draw the top card and resolve its effects immediately
You are now ready to begin the game

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Final Girl is played in turns, and each turn has five phases in order: the Action Phase, the Planning Phase, the Killer Phase, the Panic Phase, and the Upkeep Phase. You keep playing turns until either you destroy the Killer or the Killer kills you. If both happen at the same time, you win -- the Final Girl made the ultimate sacrifice. Let's walk through each phase.

walkthrough.title: The Action Phase: Playing Action Cards
walkthrough.text: This is your main phase where you do things. You play Action cards from your hand to move, attack, search, rest, and more. Most Action cards require a Horror Roll to determine how successful your action is. You can keep playing Action cards until you decide to stop, run out of cards, an action forces the phase to end, or your Time marker drops below zero. Remember, you start the game with the six Zero Cost cards as your hand.

walkthrough.title: Horror Rolls
walkthrough.text: Horror Rolls are the dice mechanic at the heart of Final Girl. The number of dice you roll depends on the current Horror Level shown on the Horror Track. A result of 5 or 6 is a success. A result of 3 or 4 is a partial success -- you may discard 2 Action cards from your hand to convert it into a full success. You can do this for each die showing 3 or 4. If none of your dice result in or are converted to a success, the Horror Roll has failed. You always roll at least 1 die, even if the Horror Level says otherwise.

walkthrough.title: Resolving Action Card Results
walkthrough.text: Each Action card has three outcome lines: a double success line (2 or more successes), a single success line (exactly 1 success), and a failure line. After your Horror Roll, resolve the effects from left to right based on which outcome you achieved. Effects use icons for things like recovering health, losing health, increasing or decreasing Horror, dealing damage, gaining or losing Time, and more. Some effects are great, some are painful -- that is the tension of Final Girl.

walkthrough.title: Movement
walkthrough.text: When an action allows you to move, you can move up to the indicated number of spaces. Each move takes you to one adjacent space -- spaces connected by a line or path on the Location board. You can move to and from the Killer's space without penalty. When leaving a space with Victims, you can bring up to 2 Victims with you. However, Victims will NOT follow you into the Killer's space. If Victims are already in the Killer's space and you move out, they will follow you away.

walkthrough.title: Saving Victims
walkthrough.text: If at any point during the Action Phase you are on a green Exit space -- even just passing through -- you may save any Victims in that space. Place saved Victims on the Victim Saved spaces on your Final Girl card. Each space grants a reward when covered: gaining an Action card, reducing Horror, moving extra spaces, and so on. Once all Victim Saved spaces are filled, flip the Final Girl card to unlock her Ultimate Ability. Victims alone on an Exit space are NOT automatically saved -- they need the Final Girl to convince them to leave.

walkthrough.title: Searching for Items
walkthrough.text: Orange Search spaces on the Location board each have an associated Item deck. To search, play a Search Action card while at a Search space and make a Horror Roll. Depending on your result, you may draw one or more cards from that Item deck. Usually you keep one and return the others to the deck -- either face up on top or face down on the bottom. Items go into your Hands or Backpack slots on the Player board. If an Item deck runs out, there is nothing left to find there.

walkthrough.title: Items, Weapons, and Your Inventory
walkthrough.text: You have two hand slots and an unlimited backpack. Items with hand icons must be in your hands to be used. A two-handed item takes up both hand slots. Items in your backpack cannot be used if they require hands. You can rearrange items freely when you gain a new one, but otherwise you can only rearrange during the Upkeep Phase. Weapons have a Range value -- a weapon with range 0 can only be used when you share a space with the Killer, while range 1 means you must be exactly 1 space away. Weapons also have a Damage Modifier that adds to your attack damage.

walkthrough.title: Attacking the Killer
walkthrough.text: To attack, you must be in the same space as the Killer (unless you have a ranged weapon). Play an attack Action card like Weak Attack or Furious Strike, make a Horror Roll, and resolve the effects. Each damage icon deals 1 damage -- remove Health markers from the Killer's board. If you have a weapon and dealt at least 1 damage, add the weapon's Damage Modifier for additional damage. When only the black Final Health token remains and damage is still being applied, flip it to see if the Killer is truly dead or comes back to life.

walkthrough.title: Gaining and Losing Time
walkthrough.text: Time is your currency. Most actions cost Time -- move the Time marker down on the Time track. Some results gain you Time -- move the marker up. You start each turn with 6 Time. You can also discard Action cards from your hand at any point before the Action Phase ends to gain +1 Time each. This is critically important -- do not underestimate discarding cards for Time. If the Time marker drops below zero into the red, the Action Phase ends immediately after your current action resolves, and you cannot gain Time back into the positive.

walkthrough.title: The Horror Level
walkthrough.text: The Horror Level fluctuates throughout the game. When you see the increase Horror icon, move the Horror marker up on the track. When you see the decrease icon, move it down. This matters because the Horror Level determines how many dice you roll. If the Horror Level would go past the bottom of the track (decreasing), you gain 1 Time instead. If it would go past the top (increasing), Bloodlust increases instead. Both ends of the track have reminder icons.

walkthrough.title: The Planning Phase
walkthrough.text: After the Action Phase, you enter Planning. First, spend any remaining Time to purchase Action cards from the Action Tableau. Each card's cost is shown in the bottom right corner. You cannot buy cards that were played or discarded since the last Planning Phase, and you cannot buy if it would push Time below zero. The hand limit is 10 cards. Second, you may always take all available Zero Cost cards for free (even if Time is at or below zero), as long as you do not exceed 10 cards. Third, reset the Time marker to 6 -- any unspent Time is lost. Finally, return all cards from the discard pile back to the Action Tableau.

walkthrough.title: The Killer Phase
walkthrough.text: Now the Killer acts. First, resolve the Killer Action shown on the back of the Finale card (or the front, once the Finale has been revealed). The Killer Action tells you who the Killer targets, how it moves, and whether it attacks. Second, draw the top Terror card and resolve its effects from top to bottom. Terror cards can make the Killer move and attack again, increase Horror, add Victims to the board, or trigger other nasty effects. Once the Finale is revealed and the Terror deck is empty, you skip drawing Terror cards.

walkthrough.title: Killer Targeting and Movement
walkthrough.text: The Killer Action specifies a target: the closest Victim, the Final Girl, or whichever is closest. The Killer always targets the CLOSEST option. If tied, choose the group with the most Victims (the Final Girl is not a Victim). The Killer moves toward its target using the shortest path, moving up to its current Movement Value on the Bloodlust track. If there are multiple move icons, the Killer moves multiple times. The Killer stops as soon as it reaches its target, even if movement remains.

walkthrough.title: Killer Attacks
walkthrough.text: When the Attack icon is present, the Killer attacks a single target in its space. The damage equals the Killer's current Attack Value on the Bloodlust track. The Killer always attacks Victims before the Final Girl unless the Final Girl was specifically targeted. Each Victim has only 1 health and is killed automatically. Killed Victims go to the Dead space on the Player board, and Bloodlust increases by 1. If you are attacked, you lose health equal to the Attack Value unless you play a Reaction card to defend.

walkthrough.title: Defending with Reaction Cards
walkthrough.text: Reaction cards have blue backgrounds and can ONLY be played when you are attacked. Play the card and make a Horror Roll. Depending on the result, you may reduce or completely ignore the damage. The Retaliate card can even deal damage back to the Killer! Each Reaction card defends against a single attack. If the Killer makes multiple attacks, you need separate Reaction cards for each. You cannot use Reaction cards to protect Victims -- only yourself.

walkthrough.title: The Panic Phase
walkthrough.text: After the Killer Phase, check if any Victims will panic. A Victim panics if at least one Victim was killed this turn AND the Victim is in the same space as the Killer. The Final Girl does NOT panic. For each panicking Victim, roll a die and consult the panic numbers on the paths leading away from that space on the Location board. Move the Victim one space in the indicated direction, or leave them if the number rolled is not shown. Victims can panic into the Killer's space, even though they would not follow you there voluntarily.

walkthrough.title: The Upkeep Phase
walkthrough.text: Usually short. Do the following in order: First, check if the Terror deck is empty -- if so, reveal the Finale card. Some Finales have immediate effects and all have a new, usually nastier, Killer Action that replaces the original. If the Dark Power card is still face down when the Finale is revealed, flip the Dark Power too. Second, perform any upkeep required by Events, Items, or special rules. Third, you may rearrange Items between your Hands and Backpack. Then start a new turn.

walkthrough.title: Bloodlust and the Killer Getting Stronger
walkthrough.text: Every time a Victim is killed, Bloodlust increases by 1 on the Killer's Bloodlust track. As Bloodlust rises, the Killer's Movement Value and Attack Value increase -- it moves faster and hits harder. The Bloodlust track also has Special Effects at certain levels, like increasing Horror or revealing the Dark Power card. Once Bloodlust reaches maximum, every additional increase triggers the Final Effect repeatedly. This creates an escalating sense of dread -- the more Victims die, the more dangerous the Killer becomes.

walkthrough.title: The Final Health Token and Adrenaline Rush
walkthrough.text: When the last normal Health marker is removed and damage still needs to be applied, flip the black Final Health token. If it is blank, they are dead. If it shows health icons (1, 2, or 3), they come back to life! Replace the black token with a white one and add normal Health markers to match the number shown. The current phase immediately ends. If they die a second time with the white token, they are truly dead. Adrenaline Rush: if either the Final Girl or the Killer is down to only their Final Health token, you roll +1 die on all Horror Rolls. If both are down to their last token, you roll +2 dice.

walkthrough.title: Game End
walkthrough.text: The game ends the instant either the Final Girl or the Killer is dead. If you kill the Killer, you win! If the Killer kills you, you lose. In the rare case that both die simultaneously, the Final Girl wins -- she made the ultimate sacrifice to rid the world of the monster. There is no point scoring in the base game; it is purely a win-or-lose experience. However, the number of Victims saved and the state of the board can serve as a personal benchmark for how well you played.

Play a Search card + Horror Roll to draw Item cards
Usually keep 1, return rest to deck top (face up) or bottom (face down)

bullets: 2 hand slots + unlimited backpack
Items with hand icons must be in hands to use; 2-handed items fill both slots

Rearrange freely on item gain, otherwise only during Upkeep

bullets: Must be in Killer's space (unless ranged weapon)
Play attack Action card + Horror Roll; damage icons = 1 damage each
Add weapon Damage Modifier if you dealt at least 1 damage
Last Health marker + damage remaining = flip Final Health token

bullets: Most actions cost Time; some gain Time
Discard Action cards from hand for +1 Time each (any time before Action Phase ends)
Time below zero = Action Phase ends immediately, cannot gain Time back to positive

bullets: Determines number of dice for Horror Rolls
Decrease past bottom of track = gain 1 Time
Increase past top of track = increase Bloodlust

summary.title: The Planning Phase
bullets: Spend remaining Time to buy Action cards from Tableau (cannot buy cards played/discarded this turn)
Always take all available Zero Cost cards free (hand limit: 10)

bullets: Resolve Killer Action on Finale card (target, move, attack)
Draw top Terror card and resolve effects top to bottom
Once Finale is revealed and Terror deck empty, skip Terror card draw

bullets: Target: closest Victim, Final Girl, or whichever is closest (per Killer Action)
Ties: choose group with most Victims; still tied = use ambiguity rules
Killer moves toward target via shortest path, stops upon reaching target

bullets: Trigger: at least 1 Victim killed this turn AND Victim is in Killer's space

Final Girl does NOT panic; Victims can panic into the Killer's space

bullets: Terror deck empty = reveal Finale card (and Dark Power if still hidden)

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Your First Game
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first turn of Final Girl. Since this is a solo game, you are the only player -- you are the Final Girl. We will use a generic setup, so the specific Killer and Location do not matter. Just follow along with the moves and decisions. By the end, you will have seen all five phases of a turn and learned from a few common mistakes. Let's go.

walkthrough.title: Your Starting Situation
walkthrough.text: You have 6 Time on the Time Track, your starting hand of 6 Zero Cost Action cards (Walk, Walk, Focus, Focus, Weak Attack, Short Rest), and your Final Girl is standing on the Location board a couple spaces away from the Killer. There are Victims scattered around the map, some near the Killer, some near Exit spaces. The Horror Level is at 3, meaning you roll 2 dice for Horror Rolls. The Killer has not moved yet. Take a breath -- you have got this.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase: First Move
walkthrough.text: Let's start your Action Phase. You want to get to a Search space to find a weapon, and there is one two spaces away. Play your Walk card and make a Horror Roll. You roll two dice and get a 4 and a 5. That is one success and one partial success. You could discard two Action cards to convert the partial, but one success is good enough -- it lets you move up to 2 spaces. Move to the Search space. The Walk card also costs you 1 Time, so move your Time marker down from 6 to 5.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase: Searching
walkthrough.text: Now you are on a Search space. Play your Focus card to... wait. Focus is not a Search card. Here is your first mistake. You do not have a Search card in your starting hand because Search is not a Zero Cost card. You need to buy one from the Action Tableau during the Planning Phase. For now, you cannot search. This is a very common mistake -- new players head to a Search space without having the right card in hand. Let's do something else instead.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Check Your Hand Before You Move
walkthrough.text: Before you move toward a Search space, make sure you actually have a Search card. Before you move toward the Killer, make sure you have an attack card and ideally a weapon. Your starting hand has Walk, Focus, Weak Attack, and Short Rest -- no Search and no Furious Strike. Plan your purchases during the Planning Phase before committing to a strategy. You wasted movement getting here without the right tools.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase: Using Focus
walkthrough.text: Since you cannot search, let's play a Focus card instead. Focus lets you plan ahead. You roll two dice and get a 6 and a 2. One success! The single success line on Focus might let you reduce Horror by 1 or gain Time. Let's say it gives you 1 Time, moving the marker back up from 5 to 6. Not a wasted turn after all. But now you have used two Action cards (Walk + Focus), and you have four left.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase: Saving Time for Planning
walkthrough.text: You have 4 cards left and 6 Time. You could keep playing cards, but here is a strategic choice. If you end the Action Phase now, you will have 6 Time to spend buying cards in the Planning Phase. That Search card costs 2 Time and a Sprint costs 2 Time. You could buy both and be much better equipped for next turn. Sometimes the smartest move is to stop early and invest in better cards. Let's end the Action Phase voluntarily with 4 Time remaining.

walkthrough.title: The Planning Phase
walkthrough.text: Now you are in the Planning Phase. You have 4 Time left. Look at the Action Tableau. You spot the Search card for 2 Time and the Sprint card for 2 Time. Spend 2 Time to buy Search, then 2 Time to buy Sprint. Your Time marker is now at 0. You also pick up any Zero Cost cards that are in the Tableau -- but both your Walks and Focuses were played this turn, so they are in the discard pile and cannot be purchased yet. Now reset Time to 6 and return all your played/discarded cards to the Tableau. Your hand now has 4 remaining starter cards plus Search and Sprint -- 6 cards total.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Forgetting to Pick Up Zero Cost Cards
walkthrough.text: Wait -- did you remember to take the Zero Cost cards from the Tableau before resetting? A very common mistake is forgetting that Zero Cost cards are always free to take. If there are any Zero Cost cards sitting in the Tableau that you did NOT play this turn, grab them now. They cost nothing and do not count against your Time. Missing free cards means you go into the next turn with fewer options. Always scan the Tableau for free pickups.

walkthrough.title: The Killer Phase
walkthrough.text: Now the Killer acts. Flip the back of the Finale card and check the Killer Action. Let's say it shows: target the closest Victim, move, attack. The Killer's Movement Value is 1 and Attack Value is 2 (Bloodlust is still low). The closest Victim is one space away, so the Killer moves there and attacks. The Victim has only 1 health and dies instantly. Place the dead Victim on the Dead space on your Player board. Bloodlust increases by 1 -- slide the marker up. Check if there is a Special Effect at this new Bloodlust level.

walkthrough.title: The Killer Phase: Terror Card
walkthrough.text: Now draw the top Terror card. Let's say it shows 'Increase Horror by 1' at the top, then a Killer Action: target closest Victim, move, attack. First, increase Horror by 1 -- move the Horror marker up. Your Horror Level is now 4, which means you still roll 2 dice for Horror Rolls (check your specific track to confirm). Then the Killer targets the closest Victim again. If there is one nearby, the Killer moves and attacks. Another Victim dies, another Bloodlust increase. Things escalate fast.

walkthrough.title: The Panic Phase
walkthrough.text: At least one Victim was killed this turn, so we check for panic. Are any surviving Victims in the same space as the Killer? If yes, roll a die for each one and consult the panic numbers on the paths leaving that space. Let's say two Victims are with the Killer. You roll a 3 and a 5. Check the board: the 3 leads east, the 5 leads north. One Victim moves east, the other moves north. They scatter -- and sometimes they panic right into danger. That is the horror movie experience.

walkthrough.title: The Upkeep Phase
walkthrough.text: Check the Terror deck -- are there cards remaining? Yes, plenty. So no Finale reveal yet. Check for any Event or special rules that require upkeep. If nothing applies, you may rearrange Items between your Hands and Backpack. You do not have any Items yet, so this is quick. Your first turn is complete.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Not Planning for the Killer Phase
walkthrough.text: Looking back, two Victims died during the Killer Phase and Bloodlust jumped twice. The Killer is now faster and hits harder. A common beginner mistake is playing the Action Phase as if only your actions matter, forgetting that the Killer Phase is coming and things will get worse. Before you end your Action Phase, ask yourself: where is the Killer going to go next? Can I move Victims away from danger? Should I position near an Exit to save someone before the Killer gets them? Anticipate the horror -- do not just react to it.

walkthrough.title: Turn 2 Preview
walkthrough.text: You start Turn 2 with 6 Time, a hand that now includes Search and Sprint, and the Killer is already getting dangerous. This turn you should use Sprint to reach a Search space, then use Search to find a weapon. Without a weapon, your Weak Attack only deals 1 damage at best -- and the Killer might have 10+ health. You need items to stand any chance. Prioritize finding a weapon within the first two turns.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have walked through a complete first turn of Final Girl. You saw all five phases in action, learned that you need to buy the right Action cards before heading to a Search space, and discovered that the Killer Phase can be devastating if you do not plan for it. Most importantly, you experienced the tension -- every Horror Roll matters, every Victim lost makes the Killer stronger, and Time is always running out.

walkthrough.title: Before You Face the Killer
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First -- check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second -- reference the Appendix for a quick guide to all game terms, card types, and icons.

And most importantly -- ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Survive out there!

summary.title: Welcome to Your First Game
bullets: Solo scripted tutorial covering one complete first turn
Works with any Killer and Location combination
Follow along with the moves -- you will see all five phases

summary.title: Your Starting Situation
bullets: 6 Time, 6 Zero Cost Action cards in hand (Walk x2, Focus x2, Weak Attack, Short Rest)
Horror Level starts at the Killer's starting value (typically 3 = 2 dice)
Victims scattered around the map, Killer a couple spaces away

summary.title: Action Phase: First Move
bullets: Play Walk card, make Horror Roll (2 dice at Horror Level 3)
1 success = move up to 2 spaces toward a Search space
Walk costs 1 Time, marker drops from 6 to 5

summary.title: Action Phase: Searching
bullets: MISTAKE: Arrived at Search space but no Search card in hand
Search is not a Zero Cost card -- must be purchased from the Tableau
Cannot search without the right card, turn partially wasted

summary.title: Lesson: Check Your Hand Before You Move
bullets: Always verify you have the right Action card before committing to movement
Starting hand: Walk, Focus, Weak Attack, Short Rest -- no Search or Sprint
Plan card purchases in the Planning Phase before committing to a strategy

summary.title: Action Phase: Using Focus
bullets: Play Focus since Search is unavailable
Horror Roll with 1 success: may gain 1 Time or reduce Horror
Not a wasted turn, but not the ideal plan

summary.title: Action Phase: Saving Time for Planning
bullets: End Action Phase early with 4 Time remaining
More Time = more cards purchased during Planning
Sometimes stopping early is the smartest play

summary.title: The Planning Phase
bullets: Spend 2 Time on Search, 2 Time on Sprint (Time drops to 0)
Pick up any available Zero Cost cards from Tableau (free)
Reset Time to 6; return all played/discarded cards to Tableau

summary.title: Mistake: Forgetting to Pick Up Zero Cost Cards
bullets: MISTAKE: Zero Cost cards in the Tableau are always free to take
Do not forget to grab them every Planning Phase
Missing free cards = fewer options next turn

summary.title: The Killer Phase
bullets: Resolve Killer Action on Finale card: target, move, attack
Killer kills a Victim (1 health), Bloodlust increases by 1
Check for Special Effects at new Bloodlust level

summary.title: The Killer Phase: Terror Card
bullets: Draw Terror card, resolve effects top to bottom
May increase Horror, then trigger another Killer Action
Another Victim killed = another Bloodlust increase; escalation is fast

summary.title: The Panic Phase
bullets: Victims in the Killer's space panic if any Victim was killed this turn
Roll die per panicking Victim, consult panic numbers on board paths
Victims scatter in random directions -- sometimes into danger

summary.title: The Upkeep Phase
bullets: Check if Terror deck is empty (if so, reveal Finale)
Perform any Event/special rule upkeep
Rearrange Items between Hands and Backpack; turn complete

summary.title: Mistake: Not Planning for the Killer Phase
bullets: MISTAKE: Forgot to anticipate Killer movement and Victim deaths
Two Victims died, Bloodlust jumped twice -- Killer is now faster and stronger
Before ending your Action Phase, ask: where will the Killer go next?

summary.title: Turn 2 Preview
bullets: Now have Search and Sprint cards for next turn
Priority: reach a Search space and find a weapon
Without a weapon, Weak Attack only deals 1 damage vs. Killer's 10+ health

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: You have completed a full first turn with all five phases
Key lessons: buy the right cards before moving, plan for the Killer Phase
Every Horror Roll matters, every Victim lost empowers the Killer

summary.title: Before You Face the Killer
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics
Reference the Appendix for game terms and icons
Ask your personal GM Guide anytime via the Q&A tab

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the biggest mistakes new Final Girl players make. First, rushing to attack the Killer without a weapon -- you will barely scratch them with a base Weak Attack. Second, ignoring Victims entirely and letting Bloodlust spiral out of control. Third, spending all your Time during the Action Phase and having nothing left to buy cards in Planning. Fourth, forgetting that you can discard Action cards for +1 Time each -- this is one of the most important tactics in the game. Fifth, not picking up free Zero Cost cards during Planning. Keep these in mind and you will survive much longer.

walkthrough.title: Time Management Is Everything
walkthrough.text: Time is the lifeblood of Final Girl. You start each turn with 6 and spend it on actions, but you also spend remaining Time buying cards in the Planning Phase. The tension is real: play more actions now, or save Time to buy better cards for future turns. In the early game, it is often better to end the Action Phase early and invest in strong cards like Search, Sprint, and Furious Strike. In the late game, you want to spend every second of Time on actions to finish the Killer off. The most common beginner mistake is blowing all Time on actions and entering Planning broke.

walkthrough.title: Get a Weapon Fast
walkthrough.text: Your base Weak Attack does pitiful damage. The Killer often has 10 to 15 health. Without a weapon's Damage Modifier, you are chipping away 1 or 2 health at a time while the Killer is slaughtering Victims and getting stronger. Your first priority should be getting to a Search space and finding a weapon. Buy a Search card in your first Planning Phase, Sprint to the Search space, and search. A weapon with +2 or +3 damage changes the entire game. Until you have a weapon, avoid the Killer unless absolutely necessary.

walkthrough.title: Balance Victim Saving and Killer Fighting
walkthrough.text: You do not have to save every Victim, and frankly you cannot. But letting too many die means Bloodlust skyrockets, and the Killer becomes a freight train. Saving Victims gives you card rewards and works toward unlocking your Ultimate Ability, which can be game-changing. A good balance is to save Victims when they are conveniently near Exit spaces and you have movement to spare. Do not go out of your way to rescue someone across the map while the Killer is breathing down your neck. Sometimes sacrifices are necessary -- this is a horror game, after all.

walkthrough.title: Discard Cards for Time Strategically
walkthrough.text: This is the tip the rulebook itself marks as CRITICAL. At any point before the Action Phase ends, you can discard Action cards from your hand for +1 Time each. This is powerful for two reasons. First, it lets you extend your Action Phase when you are doing something crucial, like a multi-card attack sequence. Second, extra Time means more card purchases in Planning. Do not be precious about your cards -- if you have a Walk or Focus that you do not need this turn, discard it for Time. The card comes back to the Tableau next Planning Phase anyway.

walkthrough.title: Manage the Horror Level
walkthrough.text: The Horror Level determines how many dice you roll, and more dice means a better chance of success. But Horror also represents how terrifying things are getting. Horror increases through card effects and escalating Bloodlust, and decreases through successful actions and saving Victims. Keep an eye on the Horror Track boundaries. If Horror would decrease past the bottom of the track, you gain 1 Time instead -- free bonus. If it would increase past the top, Bloodlust goes up -- very bad. Try to keep Horror in the mid-range where you have enough dice without risking Bloodlust overflow.

walkthrough.title: Reaction Cards Save Lives
walkthrough.text: Reaction cards like Guard and Retaliate are your lifeline when the Killer attacks. Keep at least one in your hand whenever the Killer is nearby. Guard can reduce or completely negate damage. Retaliate can deal damage back to the Killer while also protecting you. The key detail: each Reaction card only blocks one attack. If the Killer attacks twice, you need two Reaction cards. Buy Reaction cards from the Tableau whenever you plan to be near the Killer. Going in without one is asking to get flattened.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Terror Deck
walkthrough.text: The Terror deck is your game clock. There are exactly 10 Terror cards, and one is drawn each turn during the Killer Phase. When the deck runs out, the Finale is revealed -- and the Killer usually gets nastier. Count the remaining Terror cards and plan accordingly. If there are 3 cards left, you have roughly 3 turns before the Finale. Make sure you have a weapon and enough health by then. Some players try to rush the Killer down before the Finale, while others prefer to prepare and face the final form head-on. Either way, do not let the Finale catch you off guard.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't attack the Killer without a weapon -- base damage is too low
Don't ignore Victims -- uncontrolled Bloodlust makes the Killer unstoppable
Don't spend all Time on actions -- save some for Planning Phase purchases
Don't forget to discard cards for +1 Time each (critical tactic!)
Don't skip picking up free Zero Cost cards in Planning

summary.title: Time Management Is Everything
bullets: 6 Time per turn: split between Action Phase and Planning Phase purchases
Early game: end Action Phase early, invest in strong cards
Late game: spend all Time on actions to finish the Killer
Biggest mistake: entering Planning with 0 Time and no new cards

summary.title: Get a Weapon Fast
bullets: Base Weak Attack = pitiful damage vs. 10-15 Killer health
Buy Search card Turn 1, find a weapon Turn 2
Weapon Damage Modifier (+2 or +3) changes the entire game
Avoid the Killer until you are armed

summary.title: Balance Victim Saving and Killer Fighting
bullets: You cannot save everyone -- save Victims near Exit spaces when convenient
Saving unlocks card rewards and eventually Ultimate Ability
Too many dead Victims = Bloodlust spirals, Killer becomes a freight train
Strategic sacrifice is sometimes necessary

summary.title: Discard Cards for Time Strategically
bullets: Discard any Action card from hand for +1 Time (before Action Phase ends)
Extends your Action Phase for crucial plays
Extra Time = more card purchases in Planning
Discarded cards return to Tableau next Planning Phase -- not a permanent loss

summary.title: Manage the Horror Level
bullets: Horror Level = number of dice rolled; higher = more dice = better odds
Decrease past bottom = gain 1 Time (free bonus)
Increase past top = Bloodlust increase (very bad)
Keep Horror in mid-range: enough dice without risking Bloodlust overflow

summary.title: Reaction Cards Save Lives
bullets: Guard reduces/negates damage; Retaliate can damage the Killer back
Each Reaction card blocks only 1 attack -- need multiples for multi-attack
Always keep at least 1 Reaction card when near the Killer
Buy Reaction cards before engaging the Killer

summary.title: Watch the Terror Deck
bullets: 10 Terror cards = roughly 10 turns before the Finale
Count remaining cards and plan around the Finale reveal
Have a weapon and enough health before the Finale triggers
Finale = nastier Killer Action for the rest of the game

walkthrough.title: The Rush Strategy
walkthrough.text: The Rush strategy focuses on killing the Killer as fast as possible, before Bloodlust makes them unstoppable. Buy attack cards and Search cards early. Find a high-damage weapon quickly, then charge straight at the Killer. Use Furious Strike with weapon modifiers to deal massive damage in a single turn. The risk is that you might not have enough health or Reaction cards to survive the counterattack. This strategy works best against Killers with lower health or when the Location has strong weapons available early.

walkthrough.title: The Savior Strategy
walkthrough.text: The Savior strategy prioritizes rescuing Victims to unlock your Final Girl's Ultimate Ability before engaging the Killer. Move aggressively toward Victims, escort them to Exit spaces, and collect the rewards. Saving Victims also keeps Bloodlust low, which means the Killer stays weaker longer. Once the Ultimate Ability is unlocked, you often have a significant advantage in the final confrontation. The risk is that while you are playing escort, the Killer is roaming free and the Terror deck is ticking down.

walkthrough.title: The Card Economy Engine
walkthrough.text: Advanced players treat the Action Tableau like a deck-building system. In the early game, invest heavily in buying powerful cards: Furious Strike, Search, Sprint, Guard, and Retaliate. Use the discard-for-Time mechanic aggressively -- sacrifice weaker cards to buy stronger ones. By Turn 3 or 4, your hand should be stacked with powerful cards and you can execute devastating multi-card turns. The key insight is that Zero Cost cards are your Time generators -- play them early, then discard leftover weak cards for bonus Time to fuel purchases.

walkthrough.title: Optimizing Horror Rolls
walkthrough.text: Horror Rolls are the core mechanic, and understanding probability is a huge advantage. At Horror Level 3, you roll 2 dice. The chance of at least one 5 or 6 is about 55 percent. At Horror Level 5, with 3 dice, it jumps to about 70 percent. Partial successes (3 or 4) can be converted by discarding 2 cards each, so always weigh whether the converted result is worth the card cost. Save your card conversions for high-impact rolls, like Furious Strike attacks, rather than wasting them on Walk cards.

walkthrough.title: Positioning and Map Control
walkthrough.text: The Location board is a web of interconnected spaces, and your position matters enormously. Stay one or two spaces away from the Killer when possible -- close enough to attack on your turn but far enough that the Killer has to spend movement to reach you. Learn the panic numbers on each space; sometimes you can position Victims so they panic toward Exit spaces rather than toward the Killer. Use Sprint to cover large distances efficiently, and use the Killer's targeting logic against it -- if you know it will target the closest Victim, position yourself on the opposite side of the map to move freely.

walkthrough.title: Timing the Finale
walkthrough.text: The Finale reveals when the Terror deck runs out, typically around Turn 10. Experienced players plan their game around this milestone. Before the Finale, you want a weapon, enough health to survive a few hits, at least one Reaction card, and ideally your Ultimate Ability. Some Finales have devastating immediate effects -- being prepared is essential. Once the Finale is revealed, the Killer gets a new, nastier Killer Action but you no longer draw Terror cards. This can actually be beneficial: no more surprise Terror card effects. Time your final assault for right after the Finale reveal when you know exactly what you are dealing with.

walkthrough.title: Bloodlust Manipulation
walkthrough.text: Bloodlust is the single most dangerous escalation mechanic. Each Victim death increases it, and the Killer gets faster and stronger with each level. Advanced players actively manage Bloodlust by protecting key Victim clusters. If you see a group of 3 Victims near the Killer, that is a potential 3 Bloodlust increase in one Killer Phase. Move those Victims, position yourself to block the Killer, or use Pepper Spray to end the Killer Phase early. Pay special attention to the Dark Power reveal threshold on the Bloodlust track -- reaching it can dramatically change the game.

walkthrough.title: Adrenaline Rush Exploitation
walkthrough.text: When either the Final Girl or the Killer is down to only their Final Health token, you get +1 die on all Horror Rolls. If both are at Final Health, you get +2 dice. This is a massive boost. Some advanced players intentionally take damage or avoid healing to trigger their own Adrenaline Rush, then use the extra dice to land devastating attacks. This is high-risk, high-reward -- if your Final Health token is blank, you are dead. But if you have whittled the Killer down to their Final Health token too, the +2 dice bonus makes your attacks extremely likely to succeed.

walkthrough.title: Item Synergy and Loadout Planning
walkthrough.text: Your two hand slots and unlimited backpack create interesting loadout decisions. A melee weapon (range 0, high damage) pairs well with Reaction cards since you will be in the Killer's space. A ranged weapon (range 1+) pairs well with Sprint for hit-and-run tactics. Some Items have specific Action card requirements -- the Old Revolver only modifies Weak Attack, for example. When searching, think about what your current loadout needs. Two one-handed Items give you flexibility (weapon + utility), while a two-handed weapon gives you maximum damage but costs flexibility.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Board State
walkthrough.text: Every turn, before you play your first Action card, take a moment to assess the full board state. Where is the Killer? Where are the largest Victim clusters? How close are Victims to Exit spaces? What is the Bloodlust level, and how close is it to the next Special Effect? How many Terror cards remain? What is your health versus the Killer's health? This mental snapshot should drive every decision you make. The best Final Girl players do not just react -- they anticipate what the Killer Phase will do and pre-position to minimize damage and maximize opportunities.

summary.title: The Rush Strategy
bullets: Focus on killing the Killer fast before Bloodlust escalates
Buy attack cards + Search cards early, find a weapon, charge in
Best against low-health Killers or Locations with strong early weapons
Risk: insufficient health/Reaction cards for counterattacks

summary.title: The Savior Strategy
bullets: Prioritize rescuing Victims to unlock Ultimate Ability
Saving Victims = rewards + keeping Bloodlust low
Ultimate Ability gives a major edge in the final confrontation
Risk: Terror deck ticks down while you play escort

summary.title: The Card Economy Engine
bullets: Treat Action Tableau like a deck-builder: buy powerful cards early
Use discard-for-Time aggressively to fund purchases
Zero Cost cards = Time generators (play early, discard weak cards for bonus Time)
By Turn 3-4 your hand should be stacked for devastating multi-card turns

summary.title: Optimizing Horror Rolls
bullets: 2 dice: ~55% chance of at least 1 success; 3 dice: ~70%
Partial success conversion costs 2 cards per die -- save for high-impact rolls
Furious Strike conversions are worth the card cost; Walk conversions usually are not
Adrenaline Rush dice make mid-range Horror Levels much more reliable

summary.title: Positioning and Map Control
bullets: Stay 1-2 spaces from the Killer: close enough to attack, far enough to avoid hits
Learn panic numbers -- position Victims so they panic toward Exits
Use Killer targeting logic: if it targets closest Victim, position on the opposite side
Sprint covers large distances for hit-and-run plays

summary.title: Timing the Finale
bullets: Finale reveals when Terror deck runs out (~Turn 10)
Pre-Finale checklist: weapon, health, Reaction card, ideally Ultimate Ability
Post-Finale: no more Terror cards (fewer surprises), but nastier Killer Action
Time your final assault for right after the Finale reveal

summary.title: Bloodlust Manipulation
bullets: Each Victim death = Bloodlust +1 = Killer faster and stronger
Protect large Victim clusters (3 Victims near Killer = potential +3 Bloodlust)
Use Pepper Spray or positioning to block mass Victim deaths
Watch the Dark Power reveal threshold on the Bloodlust track

Some players intentionally take damage to trigger the bonus
High-risk, high-reward: blank Final Health token = instant death

summary.title: Item Synergy and Loadout Planning
bullets: Melee weapon (range 0) + Reaction cards = close combat loadout
Ranged weapon (range 1+) + Sprint = hit-and-run tactics
Some Items restrict which Action cards they modify (e.g., Old Revolver + Weak Attack only)
Two 1-handed Items = flexibility; one 2-handed weapon = max damage

summary.title: Reading the Board State
bullets: Before each turn: assess Killer position, Victim clusters, Exit proximity
Check Bloodlust level and distance to next Special Effect
Count remaining Terror cards to estimate turns until Finale
Anticipate the Killer Phase and pre-position to minimize damage

entries.definition: The first phase of each turn where the Final Girl plays Action cards to move, attack, search, rest, and take other actions. Ends when the player chooses to stop, runs out of cards, an action forces the phase to end, or the Time marker drops below zero.
The second phase of each turn. Spend remaining Time to purchase Action cards from the Tableau, take free Zero Cost cards, reset Time to 6, and return all played/discarded cards to the Tableau.

The third phase of each turn. Resolve the Killer Action on the Finale card (targeting, moving, attacking), then draw and resolve the top Terror card. Once the Finale is revealed and the Terror deck is empty, skip the Terror card draw.

The fourth phase of each turn. If at least one Victim was killed this turn, all Victims in the Killer's space panic. Roll a die for each and move them according to the panic numbers on the Location board. The Final Girl does NOT panic.
The fifth and final phase of each turn. Reveal the Finale if the Terror deck is empty, perform any required Event/Item upkeep, and optionally rearrange Items between Hands and Backpack.

The dice roll used to resolve most Action cards and some other effects. Roll a number of dice equal to the current Horror Level. Results of 5 or 6 are successes. Results of 3 or 4 are partial successes that can be converted by discarding 2 Action cards each. No successes = failure. Always roll at least 1 die.

Shown on the Horror Track on the Player board. Determines how many dice are rolled for Horror Rolls. Increases and decreases through card effects. Going past the bottom grants 1 Time; going past the top increases Bloodlust.

A resource tracked on the Time Track of the Player board. Starts at 6 each turn. Spent during the Action Phase by playing cards and during the Planning Phase to purchase new cards. Can be gained by discarding Action cards (+1 each) or certain card effects. Dropping below zero ends the Action Phase.

Tracked on the Killer board's Bloodlust track. Increases by 1 each time a Victim is killed. Higher Bloodlust means higher Movement Value and Attack Value for the Killer. Certain levels trigger Special Effects (Horror increases, Dark Power reveal). At maximum, each additional increase triggers the Final Effect.
A die result of 5 or 6 during a Horror Roll. Two or more successes trigger the double success line on Action cards. One success triggers the single success line.
A die result of 3 or 4 during a Horror Roll. May be converted to a full success by discarding 2 Action cards from your hand. Each partial can be converted independently.
When no dice result in or are converted to a success during a Horror Roll. The failure line on the Action card is resolved, which often has negative effects.

Cards played during the Action Phase to perform actions. Have three result tiers: double success, single success, and failure. Each has a Time cost shown in the bottom right corner. Played cards go to a discard pile and return to the Tableau at the end of the next Planning Phase.

Action cards with a cost of 0 Time, identified by a gold hourglass icon with '0'. You start the game with 6 of them. Can always be taken from the Tableau for free during Planning (even if Time is at or below zero), as long as you are under the 10-card hand limit. Includes Walk, Walk, Focus, Focus, Weak Attack, and Short Rest.

Action cards with a blue background that can ONLY be played in reaction to an attack on the Final Girl. Make a Horror Roll to potentially reduce or cancel damage. Each Reaction card defends against a single attack. Cannot be used to protect Victims. Examples: Guard, Retaliate.

Cards drawn during the Killer Phase. Have effects that resolve top to bottom, often including Killer Actions (targeting, moving, attacking), Horror increases, and special effects. The Terror deck contains exactly 10 cards. When it is empty, the Finale is revealed.

Cards that introduce thematic surprises. One is drawn at game start, and more may be drawn during play when the Event icon appears. Some have permanent ongoing effects, others are one-time. Drawing a new Event does NOT cancel previous Events. Multiple ongoing Events can be active simultaneously.

The Killer's endgame card, placed face down at the start. Has an initial Killer Action on its back and a more powerful Killer Action on its front. Revealed when the Terror deck runs out. May have immediate or ongoing effects. Once revealed, the front Killer Action replaces the initial one for the rest of the game.

A unique ability card for the Killer, placed face down below the Finale card at setup. Revealed when Bloodlust reaches the marked threshold. Grants the Killer a special ability for the rest of the game. Epic Dark Power cards (bloody border) are optional for harder difficulty.

Terror cards that give the Killer temporary abilities. Placed above the Killer board when drawn. Also extend the Killer's health by adding Health markers to the card. The Final Girl must remove Minor Dark Power health markers before removing the Killer's general Health markers. Discarded when all its Health markers are removed.

Cards found by searching at orange Search spaces. Placed in the Final Girl's Hands or Backpack. Items with hand icons must be held in Hands to be used. Backpack holds unlimited items but hand-icon items cannot be used from there. Some items are one-time use (discard to use), others have limited uses tracked with Tracking markers.
A Location-specific card drawn during setup that determines starting positions of the Final Girl meeple, Killer meeple, and Victim meeples. Kept face up for reference during the game.

Your character card placed below the Player board. Shows the Final Girl's name, health value, Victim Saved spaces with rewards, and the Ultimate Ability. Flip the card once all Victim Saved spaces are covered to unlock the Ultimate Ability.

A Zero Cost Action card. Allows movement based on Horror Roll results. Double success: move up to 2 spaces. Single success: move up to 1 space. Failure: move up to 1 space but with negative effects. Part of your starting hand.
A Zero Cost Action card. Provides utility effects based on Horror Roll results, such as reducing Horror or gaining Time. Part of your starting hand.
A Zero Cost Action card. A basic attack that deals minimal damage. Some weapons (like the Old Revolver) can only modify this card. Part of your starting hand.
A Zero Cost Action card. Allows health recovery based on Horror Roll results. Double success recovers 2 health; single success recovers 1 health. Part of your starting hand.

An Action card that lets you draw from an Item deck at your current Search space. Requires a Horror Roll. Better results let you look at more cards and choose the best one. Costs Time to purchase from the Tableau.
An Action card for fast movement. Allows more spaces of movement than Walk but often at higher Time cost or with risk of negative effects.
A powerful attack Action card. Deals more damage than Weak Attack but has a higher Time cost. Often ends the Action Phase on use. The go-to attack card for serious Killer damage.

A Reaction card (blue background). Played when attacked to reduce or negate incoming damage based on Horror Roll results. Double success: ignore all damage. Single success: reduce by 2. Failure: reduce by 1 (minimum 1 damage taken).
A Reaction card (blue background). Played when attacked. Can reduce damage AND deal damage back to the Killer based on Horror Roll results. High risk, high reward.

The display area where unpurchased Action cards are sorted by name in two rows. Cards are purchased from here during the Planning Phase. Played and discarded cards return to the Tableau at the end of the next Planning Phase.
The double-sided board for the Final Girl. Normal side and Extreme Horror Mode side. Contains the Health Track, Horror Track, Time Track, Hands and Backpack slots, and Dead Victims space.

The board for the Killer (reverse side of the Feature Film box cover). Shows starting stats, Health Track, Bloodlust Track with Movement and Attack Values, Finale and Dark Power card slots, and Minor Dark Power slots.

The map board (reverse side of the Feature Film box cover). Shows interconnected spaces including Search spaces (orange), Exit spaces (green), and named spaces. Panic numbers are printed between adjacent spaces.

Found on both the Player board and Killer board. Holds Health markers and the Final Health token. Health markers are removed when damage is taken and added when health is recovered, up to the maximum.
The circular track on the Player board where the Horror marker sits. The Horror Level determines the number of dice rolled for Horror Rolls. Has boundary effects at both ends.
Found on the Player board. Tracks the current Time resource. Starts at 6 each turn, moves down as Time is spent, moves up when Time is gained. Has a red zone below zero that ends the Action Phase.
Found on the right side of the Killer board. Tracks the Killer's escalating power. Shows Movement Value, Attack Value, Special Effects at certain levels, and the Final Effect at maximum.
A space on the Location board highlighted in orange with a magnifying glass icon. Has an associated Item deck. The Final Girl must be on this space and play a Search card to find Items.

A space on the Location board highlighted in green with a door icon. Victims can be saved when the Final Girl is on or passes through this space. Victims alone on an Exit space are NOT automatically saved.
A space connected to another by a line or path on the Location board. Movement goes from one space to an adjacent space. Visual proximity does not mean adjacency -- only connected paths count.

Numbers printed between adjacent spaces on the Location board. When a Victim panics, roll a die and check these numbers to determine which direction the Victim moves. If the rolled number is not shown, the Victim stays put.

One of 9 black tokens, 3 of which have health icons (1, 2, or 3) on the back. Placed secretly at setup -- one for the Killer, one for the Final Girl. When revealed after last Health marker is removed: blank = dead; health icon = come back to life with that many health. Replaced by a white token on revival.
Replaces the black Final Health token when a character comes back to life. If health is fully depleted again and the white token is removed, the character is truly dead with no more chances.

When either the Final Girl or the Killer is down to only their Final Health token (black or white), roll +1 additional die on all Horror Rolls. If both are at Final Health, roll +2 additional dice. Lost if health is recovered above just the token.

Yellow meeples placed on the Location board during setup. Have 1 health and are killed instantly when attacked. Dead Victims increase Bloodlust. Can be led by the Final Girl (up to 2 at a time) and saved at Exit spaces. Will not follow into the Killer's space.

White, orange, or blue meeples introduced by Events or other card effects. Treated exactly like normal Victims unless their card specifies otherwise. Follow the same movement, saving, and death rules.

The antagonist controlled by the game. Acts during the Killer Phase by targeting, moving, and attacking. Gets stronger via Bloodlust. Has a Finale card, Dark Power, and unique mechanics per Feature Film.

Lesser monsters that may accompany or replace the Killer in certain Feature Films. Have their own health, Attack Value, and Movement Value shown on reference cards. Limited to the number of Minion tokens available. Victims will follow the Final Girl into spaces with Minions (but not if the Killer is also there).

A collective term for both the Killer and Minions. Rules and card effects that say 'Enemy' apply to both. Rules that say 'Killer' apply only to the Killer, and rules that say 'Minion' apply only to Minions.

An Item card with Range and Damage Modifier values. Must be held in Hands to use. Range determines how far from the Killer you must be (0 = same space, 1 = one space away). Damage Modifier adds extra damage when you deal at least 1 damage with an Action card. If holding two one-handed weapons, choose one per attack.

A number on weapon Item cards indicating the required distance from the Killer to use the weapon. Range 0 means same space only. Range 1 or higher means that many spaces away. You must be at exactly the specified range.
A bonus on weapon Item cards that adds extra damage to attacks. Only applies when you deal at least 1 damage from an Action card. Added on top of the Action card's damage.
Two slots on the right side of the Player board for holding Items. Items with hand icons must be in a Hand slot to be used. Two-handed Items (2 hand icons) occupy both slots.
An unlimited storage area on the Player board for Items. Items in the Backpack that have hand icons cannot be used until moved to a Hand slot. Items without hand icons can be used from the Backpack.
Small markers placed on Item cards with limited uses. One marker is removed each time the Item is used. When the last marker is removed, the Item is discarded.

One of 3 boards used when a space becomes too crowded with Victim meeples. Place the matching token in the space on the Location board and move all Victims to the Overflow board. Victims are still considered in that space for gameplay purposes.
The maximum number of Action cards you can hold in your hand: 10 cards. You cannot purchase cards from the Tableau if you already have 10 cards. Zero Cost cards follow this limit as well.

A powerful ability on the back of the Final Girl card, unlocked by covering all Victim Saved spaces with rescued Victims. Can be immediate or ongoing depending on the specific Final Girl. Additional Victims saved after unlocking provide a separate, smaller reward.
The harder difficulty side of the Player board. Features a harsher Horror Track and only 5 Time per turn instead of 6. Recommended for experienced players only.

A separate expansion box containing one unique Killer and one unique Location. The box covers reverse to become the Killer board and Location board. Designed to be modular -- any Killer can be paired with any Location.
An ambiguity-resolution rule: resolve unclear situations in the worst way for the Final Girl. One of three optional rules for handling edge cases.
An ambiguity-resolution rule: resolve unclear situations in the most realistic or thematic way. One of three optional rules for handling edge cases.
An ambiguity-resolution rule: resolve unclear situations in the funniest way possible. One of three optional rules for handling edge cases.

At any point before the Action Phase ends, you may discard Action cards from your hand to gain +1 Time each. The rulebook marks this as a CRITICAL tactic. Discarded cards return to the Tableau at the end of the next Planning Phase.

The number of spaces the Killer can move in a single move action, determined by the Bloodlust marker's position on the Killer board. Increases as Bloodlust rises. Multiple move icons on a Killer Action multiply this value.

The amount of damage the Killer deals per attack, determined by the Bloodlust marker's position on the Killer board. Increases as Bloodlust rises. Each attack icon on a Killer Action triggers a separate attack at this damage value.

The sequence of targeting, moving, and attacking performed by the Killer during the Killer Phase. Found on the Finale card back (initial) and front (post-reveal), and on Terror cards. Resolved left to right.
The first part of a Killer Action that determines who the Killer pursues. Options: closest Victim, the Final Girl, or whichever is closest. Ties broken by largest Victim group, then ambiguity rules.
